feature.name: Agent OpenTelemetry Trial
feature.summary: Authenticated OpenTelemetry gateway for Codex, Claude Code, and other agent telemetry with SigNoz as the v1 backend.

assumptions: Python and FastAPI are acceptable for auth-api.
SQLite is acceptable for the team-trial control-plane store.
Docker Compose is the local runtime.
SigNoz is the only v1 backend.
Normal onboarding remains content-minimal by default.
open_questions: Choose exact Python packaging workflow before AOTEL-001 starts.
Choose whether SigNoz bootstrap wraps official Compose files or vendors a pinned copy.
Choose CI provider before CI implementation.
Choose production TLS boundary before production deployment work.

epics.title: Control plane
epics.objective: Implement users, tokens, auth verification, audit, and local operator CLI.

epics.title: Gateway path
epics.objective: Implement authenticated OTLP ingress, Collector enrichment, and Compose wiring.

epics.title: Backends and dashboards
epics.objective: Bootstrap SigNoz and starter dashboards without exposing unauthenticated ingestion.

epics.title: Client onboarding
epics.objective: Generate safe Codex and Claude Code telemetry config with explicit max-capture overlays.

epics.title: Validation and operations
epics.objective: Provide local commands, smoke tests, troubleshooting, and security checks.

tasks.title: Project scaffold and developer ergonomics
tasks.objective: Create the minimal repository scaffold needed for later Python services, CLI packages, local environment defaults, and Make targets.
non_goals: Do not implement auth logic.
Do not start Compose services.
Do not add CI yet.
implementation_steps: Create root command targets as placeholders wired to future files.
Create non-secret local environment example values.
Create Python package metadata for auth-api and otelctl.
Add ignores for local DBs, env files, caches, and SigNoz/vendor output.
tests_to_write_first: A basic packaging/import test for each Python package once files exist.
acceptance_criteria: Root Makefile contains documented targets with clear failure messages for unimplemented dependencies.
.env.example contains no secrets.
Python package metadata exists for auth-api and otelctl.
Generated local artifacts are ignored.
review_focus: No over-broad Make targets that claim functionality not implemented.
tasks.rollback_notes: Remove scaffold files; no data migration required.

tasks.title: Auth storage and token primitives
tasks.objective: Implement SQLite schema, user/token models, token generation, hashing, parsing, revocation, expiry, scopes, capture profile, and audit helpers.
non_goals: Do not implement HTTP routes.
Do not implement CLI UX.
implementation_steps: Create schema migration matching docs/token-model.md.
Implement token id parsing and random secret generation.
Store only token hashes, prefixes, and last four characters.
Implement constant-time token verification helpers.
Implement audit insert helpers without request bodies.
tests_to_write_first: Token format parse/generate tests.
Hash mismatch/revoked/expired/disabled-user tests.
Audit row creation test.
acceptance_criteria: Schema includes users, tokens, and ingest_audit fields from docs.
Token secrets are never stored in plaintext.
Validation distinguishes accepted, revoked, expired, disabled-user, and hash-mismatch states.
capture_profile defaults to normal.
tasks.rollback_notes: Revert auth storage files and migration; no production data exists yet.

tasks.title: Auth API verification endpoint
tasks.objective: Expose FastAPI verification endpoint that validates bearer tokens and returns trusted telemetry headers for Nginx auth_request.
non_goals: Do not expose public admin endpoints.
Do not implement token issuance over HTTP.
implementation_steps: Add /auth/verify route for Nginx subrequests.
Return 204 and trusted headers on valid tokens.
Return 401 or 403 according to token failure mode.
Record ingest audit details when token id is parseable.
Add Dockerfile for Compose runtime.
tests_to_write_first: Missing/malformed/unknown token returns 401.
Revoked/expired/disabled user returns 403.
Valid token returns trusted headers and 204.
acceptance_criteria: Valid requests include X-Telemetry-User, X-Telemetry-Team, X-Telemetry-User-Id, X-Telemetry-Token-Id, and X-Telemetry-Capture-Profile.
No admin issuance endpoint is public.
Audit rows do not store request bodies.
review_focus: Header names matching Nginx and Collector docs.
tasks.rollback_notes: Revert auth API files; storage primitives remain usable by CLI.

tasks.title: otelctl user and token CLI
tasks.objective: Implement local operator CLI for user creation, token issuance/list/revocation, and user disable.
non_goals: Do not add a public admin UI.
Do not print token secrets after issuance.
implementation_steps: Implement users add command.
Implement tokens issue/list/revoke commands.
Implement users disable command.
Print Codex and Claude Code snippets on issue.
Support expiry duration parsing.
tests_to_write_first: CLI creates user and issues token.
Token secret appears only once.
Revoke and disable commands change validation outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Commands match docs/token-model.md.
Token issue prints snippets from onboarding contract.
List output shows token prefix/last4 but not secret.
tasks.rollback_notes: Revert CLI files; auth storage remains.

tasks.title: Nginx authenticated OTLP ingress
tasks.objective: Add Nginx config for authenticated OTLP/HTTP ingress using auth_request and trusted telemetry headers.
non_goals: Do not implement TLS automation.
Do not expose direct Collector or SigNoz ingestion.
implementation_steps: Allow only /v1/logs, /v1/traces, and /v1/metrics.
Add /_auth internal subrequest.
Overwrite X-Telemetry-* headers from auth-api responses.
Set trusted source IP header from socket or trusted proxy chain.
Return 404 for other paths and 200 for /healthz.
tests_to_write_first: Config text test for allowed paths and auth_request.
Config text test that proxy_set_header overwrites trusted headers.
acceptance_criteria: Only documented OTLP paths proxy to Collector.
auth_request is required on every OTLP path.
Client-supplied identity headers cannot pass through.

tasks.title: Collector gateway configuration
tasks.objective: Add local and production Collector gateway configs with metadata enrichment, agent normalization, batching, queues, retry, and SigNoz export.
non_goals: Do not add custom OTLP parsing.
Do not add a secondary warehouse exporter.
implementation_steps: Configure OTLP/HTTP receiver with include_metadata true.
Map trusted metadata headers to resource attributes.
Normalize agent.tool for Codex and Claude Code.
Configure memory limiter, batch processor, sending queue, retry, and debug exporter.
Keep SigNoz export internal.
tests_to_write_first: YAML parse test.
Config assertion for include_metadata and trusted attributes.
Config assertion for sending queue and retry.
acceptance_criteria: Collector config includes all required telemetry.* attributes.
agent.capture.profile is copied from trusted metadata.
Exporter queue and retry are enabled.
review_focus: Pipeline coverage for logs, traces, and metrics.

tasks.title: Gateway Compose stack and Make targets
tasks.objective: Wire auth-api, Nginx, and Collector together with Compose and Make targets for local trial operations.
non_goals: Do not expose SigNoz or Collector OTLP ports directly to host.
Do not implement production TLS.
implementation_steps: Define auth-api, nginx, and otel-collector services.
Join gateway services to the SigNoz network without publishing internal ingestion ports.
Wire Make targets: up, down, logs, user, token, smoke, signoz-up.
Document environment variables in .env.example.
tests_to_write_first: Compose config validation.
Port exposure assertion for Collector and SigNoz ingestion.
acceptance_criteria: make up starts gateway stack.
make user and make token execute otelctl in auth-api context.
Collector and SigNoz OTLP ports are not host-published.
tasks.rollback_notes: Revert Compose, Makefile, and env changes.

tasks.title: SigNoz bootstrap
tasks.objective: Add local SigNoz bootstrap files and documentation while keeping ingestion private to the gateway network.
non_goals: Do not implement Kubernetes or Terraform.
Do not add a second warehouse.
implementation_steps: Choose wrapper or pinned official SigNoz Compose strategy.
Document startup, shutdown, UI access, and retention checks.
Keep OTLP ingestion on internal networks only.
tests_to_write_first: Compose config validation.
Port exposure assertion.
acceptance_criteria: make signoz-up can start SigNoz after Makefile integration.
SigNoz UI access is documented.
SigNoz OTLP ingestion is not exposed to teammates.

tasks.title: Smoke and security verification scripts
tasks.objective: Implement smoke scripts that send test telemetry and verify auth, header trust, source IP, and direct-ingestion protections.
non_goals: Do not depend on production endpoints.
Do not store real tokens in repo.
implementation_steps: Implement a minimal OTLP/HTTP test log sender.
Verify invalid token returns 401.
Verify all three OTLP signal paths require auth.
Verify spoofed X-Telemetry-* headers are overwritten.
Verify direct Collector and SigNoz OTLP host ports are unavailable.
Update operations docs with exact commands.
tests_to_write_first: Unit tests for CLI flags and endpoint path construction.
Integration smoke test against local Compose stack.
acceptance_criteria: Milestone 1 acceptance criteria are executable.
Valid token reaches Collector/SigNoz.
Invalid token and direct-ingestion paths fail.
tasks.rollback_notes: Remove smoke scripts and revert operations doc changes.

tasks.title: Codex onboarding generator
tasks.objective: Implement Codex config template and installer that backs up user config and applies content-minimal OTel settings.
non_goals: Do not enable prompt capture by default.
Do not rewrite unrelated Codex settings.
implementation_steps: Create parseable Codex TOML template.
Implement timestamped config backup helper.
Install or replace only the managed OTel block.
Support endpoint and token arguments.
Keep max-capture prompt logging as explicit overlay.
tests_to_write_first: TOML template parse test.
Installer preserves unrelated config.
Installer creates timestamped backup.
acceptance_criteria: Existing ~/.codex/config.toml is backed up.
Normal profile sets log_user_prompt=false.
Rendered TOML parses.
tasks.rollback_notes: Remove Codex template and installer scripts.

tasks.title: Claude Code onboarding generator
tasks.objective: Implement Claude Code env templates and installer with content-minimal defaults and explicit max-capture profile.
non_goals: Do not modify shell startup files by default.
Do not enable raw API body logging by default.
implementation_steps: Create normal Claude env template.
Create max-capture env overlay.
Implement installer that writes env file and prints source command.
Support endpoint, token, and profile arguments.
tests_to_write_first: Shell syntax test for generated env.
Installer normal profile excludes prompt/tool/raw body capture.
Max profile includes explicit capture flags.
acceptance_criteria: Normal env includes OTLP exporters and bearer auth.
Normal env excludes prompt/tool/raw body capture.
Max env includes explicit high-capture flags.

tasks.title: Starter SigNoz dashboards
tasks.objective: Add initial SigNoz dashboard JSON stubs and import docs for team usage, Codex, Claude, tool usage, and Collector health.
non_goals: Do not optimize queries before live data exists.
Do not group metrics by high-cardinality fields by default.
implementation_steps: Create dashboard JSON files for documented starter set.
Use team/user/tool/model/capture filters.
Avoid session id, branch, command text, and file paths as default metric group-bys.
Document manual import limitations if SigNoz schema is not finalized.
tests_to_write_first: JSON syntax validation.
Static scan for prohibited high-cardinality default group-bys.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard files exist for Codex, Claude, team usage, tool usage, and Collector health.
Dashboards include telemetry.user.email, telemetry.team.id, agent.tool, and agent.capture.profile filters.
Collector health dashboard includes exporter queue/failure indicators.

waves.parallel_safety: Write sets are disjoint by exact file path; shared parent directories are creation-only.
Both depend on auth storage but write API and CLI files separately.
Single integration task after dependent service/config tasks.
Final smoke/security verification after Compose and client artifacts exist.
